title: Dense French Beef Bourguignon
description: A hearty, comforting beef stew originating from France, flavored with herbs and simmered in red wine. Perfect for a cozy brunch or dinner.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the beef until browned on all sides, then remove it from the pot and set it aside.
Reduce heat to medium-low and add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the pot.
Add the sliced onions and cook until they are softened and lightly caramelized, about 15-20 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the sliced carrots and celery and cook for 2-3 minutes, until they start to soften.
Add the button mushrooms and cook for 2-3 minutes, until they release their liquid and start to brown.
Add the red wine and beef broth to the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the beef to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2-3 hours, or until the beef is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Season with fresh thyme and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh thyme, if desired.
Serve with crusty bread or over egg noodles, if desired.
Optional: for an added crispy crust, cook the dish in a skillet with a small amount of oil until the bottom is golden brown.
